name: Scallion Pancakes #1
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the flour and sugar. Stir in the boiling water, mixing just until the water is absorbed.
2. Gradually stir in enough cold water so that a dough forms and pulls away from the sides of the bowl; it should no longer be sticky.
3. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 3-5 minutes, or until smooth and elastic. Cover loosely with a dampened cloth and let rest for 1 hour.
4. Redust the surface with flour and knead the dough again for several minutes, or until smooth.
5. Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and cover with a dampened cloth. Using a floured rolling pin, roll one piece of dough into a 6-7 inch round.
6. Lightly brush the round with sesame oil. Evenly scatter some salt and scallions on the round, then roll up tightly into a cylinder.
7. Coil the cylinder around itself into a spiral, pinching the end under into the dough. Repeat with the remaining dough pieces. Cover coils with a dampened cloth and let rest 15-20 minutes.
8. Using a floured rolling pin, roll each coiled dough on a lightly floured surface into 6-7 inch rounds.
9. Heat peanut oil in a wok or heavy skillet over medium-high heat to 375°F. Carefully add a pancake and fry 1-2 minutes per side, or until golden.
10. As each pancake fries, press the center lightly with a metal spatula to ensure it is cooked through. Remove to a plate lined with paper towels to drain.
11. Repeat with remaining pancakes. Cut each pancake into 6-8 wedges. Serve immediately.
Serves 4-6 as part of a multi-course meal.
Origin: Cookbook Digest, Mar/April 93
Shared by: Sharon Stevens.
